idea.hook: A Moon with RAIN... but it's NOT water? 🌧️🤯
facts: Titan, Saturn's largest moon, is the only moon in our solar system with a thick atmosphere, with surface pressure about 1.5 times Earth's!
Beneath its orange haze, Titan hosts vast rivers, lakes, and seas... but they're filled with liquid methane and ethane, not water.
This alien world even has a complete methane weather cycle, including clouds, rain, and evaporation.
idea.payoff: Explore a chilling Earth-like world where fuel falls from the sky and flows in rivers!
idea.title: Titan: Rain That Burns! 🔥🌧️🪐

scenes.text: A Moon with RAIN... but it's NOT water? 🌧️🤯
scenes.visual: A stylized animation of rain falling, then a mysterious, orange-hued moon silhouette appears behind the rain. A subtle '?' flashes.

scenes.text: Titan: Moon with a THICK Atmosphere! (1.5x Earth's pressure!)
scenes.visual: An animated depiction of Saturn's moon Titan, clearly showing a dense, orange atmosphere enveloping it. Subtle pressure gauge graphic appears.

scenes.text: Rivers, Lakes, Seas... of LIQUID METHANE & ETHANE!
scenes.visual: An animated landscape of Titan, showcasing vast rivers, lakes, and seas with a reflective, dark liquid surface. Emphasize the 'not water' visually.

scenes.text: Complete METHANE Weather Cycle: Clouds, Rain, Evaporation!
scenes.visual: An infographic animation illustrating the methane cycle: clouds forming, rain falling into a liquid lake, and then vapor rising from the surface.

scenes.text: Explore Titan: Where FUEL falls from the sky!
scenes.visual: A dramatic animated flyover of Titan's surface, revealing vast, dark methane oceans under an alien sky with distant Saturn visible. Emphasize the 'fuel' with a subtle glow.
